speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Let's start with your vital signs—I'm seeing your diastolic blood pressure is 102 mmHg today.

speaker: patient
text: Good morning, Doctor. Yeah, I've been feeling a bit off lately—kind of fatigued and lightheaded sometimes.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for sharing that. Have you noticed any dizziness when standing up, or any chest discomfort?

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate is 70 bpm—steady and within normal range.

speaker: doctor
text: Body temperature is 36.8 °C—completely normal.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, I’ve had this dull headache almost every morning this week—mostly behind my eyes.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your elevated diastolic pressure and persistent headache, the working diagnosis is Essential Hypertension.

speaker: patient
text: Hypertension? But my blood pressure was fine last year—what could have changed?

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin level is 160 g/L—well within the normal range for an adult male.

speaker: patient
text: I haven’t been taking my home BP cuff seriously, but I did check it twice yesterday—it was 158 over 104.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s consistent with what we’re seeing. We’ll need to monitor closely and consider lifestyle adjustments first.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve cut back on salt and started walking daily—but the headaches haven’t improved much.

speaker: doctor
text: We’ll also run some labs to rule out secondary causes—renal function, electrolytes, and repeat CBC in a few days.

speaker: patient
text: I’m a little worried—I read online that low hemoglobin can cause headaches too.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s a good point, but your initial hemoglobin was solid at 160 g/L, so anemia isn’t likely driving this.

speaker: doctor
text: However, your follow-up hemoglobin came back at 149 g/L—slightly lower, though still in the normal range.

speaker: doctor
text: Also, your heart rate today is 89 bpm—higher than the 70 we recorded earlier this morning.

speaker: patient
text: Wait—did you say 89? Because when I checked my pulse just now in the waiting room, it was exactly 70.

speaker: doctor
text: Interesting—that reading matches the one from earlier. Let me recheck manually to confirm consistency.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that number—the 70 bpm—is what I got both times: once this morning at home and just now.
